title: cassia gualanensis
intro: Cassia gualanensis, also known as Guanacaste or Cassia, is a tropical tree native to the Americas, belonging to the Fabaceae family and is characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and compound leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cassia gualanensis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting digestive health. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, although further studies are required to confirm its efficacy and safety.
